message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="1 blocking: fix validation error dead-end page">
Almost there — 7 of 8 reviewers have approved Step 7. One blocking item from Steve Krug:

**BLOCKING: Validation error dead end** — When submitting "Project" with an empty first action title, the error response shows a raw `<h1>` page with no navigation. The user is stranded. Fix: redirect back to `/inbox` with an error message, or return inline feedback.

Please fix this, run the pipeline, commit, push, and wait for CI green. Then notify Steve Krug for re-review.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Good catch from Steve Krug. The current code returns a bare `

First action title is required for projects

` with no navigation, stranding the user. I need to fix this to return inline feedback or redirect. Let me look at how the existing error handling works for the HTMX vs non-HTMX paths.

content.text:
The right approach: for HTMX requests, re-render the inbox item with an inline error message visible to the user. For non-HTMX, redirect back to `/inbox` (the item is still there since the transaction wasn't committed). I need to add an `error` field to the `InboxItemTemplate`.

Let me update the template, route handler, and add the CSS for the error message.

First, let me update the `InboxItemTemplate` struct to support an optional error message.

content.text:
The `inbox.html` template uses `{% include "inbox_item.html" %}` which means it inherits the variables from the parent template's context. Since `InboxTemplate` doesn't have an `error` field, the included `inbox_item.html` won't have access to `error`. I need to check how Askama handles this.

In Askama, `{% include %}` shares the same template struct's context. The `InboxTemplate` struct doesn't have an `error` field, but the `inbox_item.html` template references `error`. This will cause a compile error because the `InboxTemplate` doesn't have an `error` field.

I have two options:
1. Add `error: Option<String>` to `InboxTemplate` too (set to `None` always)
2. Use a different approach in the template

Let me add the field to `InboxTemplate`.

content.text: The client uses `redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none())`, so redirects are NOT followed. The response will be a 303/302 with a Location header. The test reads `response.text()` which will be the redirect body (empty), not the inbox page. I need to update the test to assert on the redirect status and then separately verify the item is still in inbox.

content.text:
All green. Full pipeline:
- cargo fmt: clean
- cargo clippy: clean
- cargo test: 70 unit + 190 integration = 260 tests, all green
- npx playwright test: 11 e2e tests, all green

Now commit and push.

input.command: git add src/routes/inbox.rs templates/inbox_item.html static/css/main.css tests/api/inbox.rs .claude-sessions/

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix validation error dead-end for clarify-as-project

When submitting "Project" with an empty first action title, the error
response was a bare <h1> page with no navigation, stranding the user.

HTMX requests now get a 422 with the inbox item re-rendered inline
including an error message ("Enter a first action for this project")
via role="alert". Non-HTMX requests redirect back to /inbox where
the item remains (transaction was never started).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.content:
Steve, I've fixed the validation error dead-end you flagged. Here's what changed: **The problem:** Submitting "Project" with an empty first action title returned a bare `

First action title is required for projects

` page with no navigation, stranding the user. **The fix:** - **HTMX path (primary):** Returns 422 with the inbox item re-rendered inline, including an error message `"Enter a first action for this project"` in a `

` element. The user stays on the inbox page with their item still visible and can correct the error immediately. - **Non-HTMX path (fallback):** Redirects back to `/inbox` (303). The item remains in the inbox since the transaction was never started (title validation fails before any DB work). **Files changed:** - `src/routes/inbox.rs` — InvalidTitle error handler now branches on HTMX vs non-HTMX - `templates/inbox_item.html` — Added conditional error message paragraph with `role="alert"` - `static/css/main.css` — Added `.inbox-item__error` styling - `tests/api/inbox.rs` — Updated existing test, added new HTMX validation error test **Test results:** 260 Rust tests + 11 Playwright e2e, all green. CI green (run 21968975698). Commit: 1a40c16 Please re-review when you get a chance.
